qubit.explorer: a magic coin that is both heads AND tails while spinning — it only picks one when you catch it
qubit.adventurer: a quantum system that exists in a superposition of states with probability amplitudes α and β
qubit.scholar: a two-level quantum system described by |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩ where |α|² + |β|² = 1
qubit.researcher: a point on the Bloch sphere parameterised as |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^(iφ)sin(θ/2)|1⟩

superposition.explorer: a cat in a box that is BOTH awake and asleep until you peek inside
superposition.adventurer: a wave function that exists in multiple states simultaneously before measurement
superposition.scholar: a linear combination of basis states with complex amplitudes that interfere
superposition.researcher: an element of a Hilbert space that evolves unitarily under the Schrödinger equation

entanglement.explorer: two magical connected stars that always mirror each other — even far apart
entanglement.adventurer: correlated quantum particles that cannot be described by independent state vectors
entanglement.scholar: a non-separable joint quantum state where measurement on one instantly determines the other
entanglement.researcher: a state with non-zero quantum discord exhibiting Bell inequality violations

interference.explorer: like two ripples in a pond — they can add together or cancel each other out
interference.adventurer: constructive and destructive wave interference that amplifies or cancels probability amplitudes
interference.scholar: amplitude interference that underlies algorithmic advantage in quantum computing
interference.researcher: phase-coherent superposition enabling quantum speedup via amplitude amplification

approved: Electrons form interference patterns when unobserved (wave behaviour)
Observation collapses the wave function — electron then behaves as a particle
The interference pattern is a probability distribution, not the electron physically splitting
Any physical measurement interaction causes wave function collapse
Superposition is a genuine physical state — not uncertainty about a hidden value
Amplitudes can be negative — probability is the squared magnitude
Superposition enables quantum interference, not parallel computation
blocked: like many computers running at once

approved: Entangled particles share a quantum state — measurement on one instantly determines the other
No information is transmitted faster than light via entanglement
Entanglement is a correlation between measurement outcomes
blocked: particles send signals to each other
